rulings.Case Title: JOHN GIDDING et al VS. IVAN KAYE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Moiton Anmd Otion Compelling Responses To Post Judgment Interrogatories, For Order Compelling Responses Topost Judgment Demand Fo Rproduction Of Documents And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 23, 2013, Line 1: Cross Complainant GLENDONBROOK WINES PTY LTD'S Motion Compelling Responses to Post Judgment Interrogatories, for Order Compelling Responses to Post Judgment Demand For Production of Documents and for Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Brad Kaplan, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to brad@kaplans.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. Hearing required. Plaintiff's counsel shall bring to the hearing the responses to the discovery which are the subject of this motion. Assuming full discovery responses are provided, the court tentativly rules as follows: Motion for terminating sanctions is denied. Motion for evidentiary sanctions is denied. Motion to compel further response to discovery is granted. Counsel should be prepared at the hearing to discuss the scope of their meet and confer efforts prior to the filing of this motion so the court can assess Defendant's request for monetary sanctions. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MARIA RAMIREZ VS. SAN FRANCISCO NETWORK MINISTRIES HOUSING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, August 23, 2013, Line 2, DEFENDANT VINCENT LEA MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant Lea's Motion for Summary Judgment as to the first and second causes of action in Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint is granted. Defendant produces sufficient evidence to meet his burden that he did not have any control over the trench in the sidewalk at 2380 Folsom and that he only installed two utility junction boxes above the sidewalk on or about February 8, 2008 and June 25, 2008. (See Declaration of Lea in Support of Motion of Summary Judgment, 1:27-2:8; Exhs. 1-5.). Furthermore, Defendant states that he never removed concrete, or excavated, dug, or worked on the pavement located in the vicinity of 2380 Folsom, nor did he place a board or platform on the sidewalk. (Id. 3:1-8.) The burden shifted to Plaintiff, who has not shown that there is a triable issue as to any material fact. Defendant Lea's purported inconsistent statement regarding the date he installed the second box does not create a triable issue as to whether Defendant Lea installed the platform or ever broke the pavement by digging or excavating at any time, let alone on or about February 8 and June 25, 2008. Plaintiff does not dispute that that Defendant does not own, control, manage, or maintain 2380 Folsom, or the sidewalks adjacent to or near it. (See Plaintiff's Opposition to Lea's Separate Statement of Undisputed Facts 23.) The Declaration of Alexander Alexeev in Opposition to Lea's Summary Judgment Motion does not create a triable issue of fact on these issues or impeach Defendant's Lea credibility. (Id. 8.) The recently taken deposition of Brandon Lea corroborates Defendant Lea's testimony that he did not excavate or work on the pavement. (Second Suppl. Decl. of Marcia Pollioni in Support of Vincent Lea's Motion for Summary Judgment, filed August 20, 2013, Ex. 2 at pp. 7-8; 16-24.) The Court declines Plaintiff's further request to deny summary judgment pursuant to CCP 437c(e). The Court continued the hearing on August 2, 2013 to allow Plaintiff to take additional discovery, and Plaintiff has shown no basis to deny the motion at this point. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: TELEKENEX, INC. VS. JEFBAR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, August 23, 2013, Line 8, DEFENDANT JEFBAR MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant's motion for summary judgment on the ground of unconscionability is denied. Unconscionability has procedural and substantive elements; "both must be appear for a court to invalidate a contract or one of its individual terms."(Serpa v. California Surety Investigations, Inc. (2013) 215 Cal. App. 4th 695, 703.) Defendant has not shown that the contract was procedurally unconscionable. Defendant could have obtained phone service from other phone providers. Currie-Martinez Dec. 12, 13, and 26. Mr. Christian is an experienced business person. (Losch Dec., Ex. L at 27:19-34:14) (Christian Dep.). His decision to stay with Telekenex as a service provider cannot be unconscionable based on the alleged conduct of the third party (SI&A) from whom Jetbar purchased its business demanding that Jetbar quickly set up its own phone service. Further, Defendant has not shown surprise; the "early termination charge" is in a paragraph titled as such in the Master Service Agreement ("MSA"), in bold and underlined. (Christian Decl. 13, Ex. A., p.3, 18.) All four pages of the MSA are initialed by Mr. Christian, and the last page is signed by him. He read it carefully before signing. (Currie-Martinez Dec., 23 and Exhs. D, E, F, G, and H.) Having failed to show procedural unconscionability, the motion is denied. In any event, even if any procedural unconscionability had been established, Defendant has not shown substantive unconscionability. The allegedly unconscionable clause is the early termination clause. Defendant has not identified facts to show that it was substantively unconscionable at the time the contract was entered into. Defendant's objections to any of the evidence cited in this order are overruled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Compliance By East West Bank With Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 23, 2013, Line 7: Plaintiffs ROSARIO O'CONNOR, JAMES O'CONNOR, O'CONNOR FAMILY LIVING TRUST DTD 03/23/2001, EMMELINE PUNSALAN, PUNSALAN FAMILY TRUST DTD 02/18/2004, C. PUNSALAN IRREVOCABLE TRUST DTD 11/12/2005, LUZVIMINDA CAYANAN, LUISA CAYANAN, CYNTHIA HERNANDEZ, ALFRED HERNANDEZ, MARTIN CALIMPUSAN, SUSAN CALIMPUSAN, EMELITA CANANEA, JONATHAN CANANEA, ANDREW CANANEA, TEOFILA GUTIERREZ, ISIDORO GUTIERREZ, THAHIR THOPPIYIL, LORNA THOPPIYIL, GLOBAL BIO-MEDICAL SUPPLY, INC., and EDERLINDA LEARY'S Motion for Order Compelling Compliance by East West Bank with Responses to Requests for Production of Documents and for Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Brad Kaplan, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. Hearing required. Counsel for Defendant should be prepared to respond to the questions raised in Plaintiff's second compliance report including: (1) whether East West Bank's search for audit reports was limited to those related to defendants; and (2) whether all manuals, handbooks and operations manuals were produced in accord with those items selected by Plaintiff. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SHIRLEY TENE VS. NANCY TAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Discovery Responses
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 23, 2013, Line 8: Defendant NANCY TAN'S Motion to Compel Discovery Responses Pro Tem Judge Brad Kaplan, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The hearing on the motion is continued to September 25, 2013 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 302. Defendant is to provide notice of the new hearing date and time, and provide copies of the moving papers to Plaintiff on or before August 28, 2013. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: VICTORIA PETERSON VS. G. MAZZERA COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant G. Mazzera Company'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Attendance At Deposition And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, August 23, 2013, Line 13, Defendant G. Mazzera Company'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Attendance At Deposition And For Monetary Sanctions. The motion to compel Plaintiff's attendance at deposition is granted. Plaintiff is to appear for deposition on September 9, 2013 or on any other mutually agreeable date no later than September 13, 2013. Request for sanctions is denied. A court reporter will not be provided for this discovery hearing. If the parties wish to have a court reporter, they must mutually agree and select one court reporter for the hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: SANDRA SALAZAR AS LEGAN GUARDIAN OF MINOR CHILD VS. UCSF BEINOFF CHILDREN'S HOSPITAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, August 23, 2013, Line 9, DEFENDANT TRISTAN SANDS, M.D., THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, SANDRIJN VAN SCHAIK, M.D. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Hearing required. No opposition was filed. At the hearing the Court will inquire into the status of counsel. A minor plaintiff must be represented by counsel; a non-attorney parent or guardian cannot represent the minor child in court without an attorney. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMBILE INSURANCE COMPANY VS. MICHAEL ANTHONY TEJADA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, August 23, 2013, Line 10, DEFENDANT MICHAEL TEJADA DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Continued to September 23, 2013 to give State Farm the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B and Mr. Tejada the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6C. State Farm must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers and Mr. Tejada must deliver courtesy copies of the operative Complaint to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than August 23, 2013 at 4:00 pm. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the moving and reply papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: RICKE ASSOCIATES PTE. LTD. VS. EXIGEN CAPITAL MANAGEMENT, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, August 23, 2013, Line 12, PLAINTIFF RICKE ASSOCIATES PTE. LTD. APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER. Hearing required. Plaintiff shall bring to the hearing: 1. a file stamped copy of his declaration showing that it was signed and dated; the Court's courtesy copy is unsigned and undated; 2. a file stamped proof of service of the summons and complaint in this action on defendant. (See CCP § 484.040 setting forth papers that must be served on defendant before hearing). If the summons and complaint have not been timely served prior to the hearing, and pursuant to CCP § 484.040, the application for right to attach order will be taken off calendar. =(302/MJM)
